message:
✨ Fix GA4 Realtime tracking for localhost and all deployments (#2182)

The custom analytics proxy (/api/m) sent events server-side to GA4's
/g/collect endpoint, which processes them for standard reports but NOT
for Realtime — GA4 requires direct browser-to-GA4 communication for
Realtime.

Fix: Load gtag.js as the primary analytics path. Events sent via
gtag('event', ...) go directly from the browser to GA4 and appear
in Realtime immediately.

Loading order with graceful fallback:
1. First-party proxy (/api/gtag) — Go backend serves gtag.js from
   same origin, bypassing domain-based ad blockers
2. Google CDN — fallback for Netlify where Go backend isn't available
3. Custom proxy (/api/m) — fallback when gtag.js is blocked entirely
   by content-based ad blockers (standard reports only, no Realtime)

Also wired up the existing GA4ScriptProxy handler (was defined but
never registered as a route) and extracted ga4RealMeasurementID()
helper to deduplicate measurement ID lookup.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
